name: Connacht (Ireland)

colors.symbolism: the silver hoist half of the historic Connacht arms
the sable fly half of the arms

description: A horizontal banner of the Connacht coat of arms: vertically divided silver and black, charged across the join with half an eagle (issuing into the silver) and an armoured arm bearing a sword (issuing into the black).
history: The arms of Connacht are documented from the late medieval period, attributed to the kings of Connacht and surviving as a heraldic banner of the province. The flag is the banner of these arms and is widely flown at sporting and cultural events.

features.symbolism: a per pale division: silver to the hoist and sable to the fly, charged with a single composite emblem spanning the divide.

a divided shield: in the silver hoist, half of a black eagle displayed; in the black fly, a vested arm in red and gold issuing from the dividing line and grasping an upright sword. Both halves are conjoined along the partition line.
